title: Living Briefing System - Continuous AI Updates
description: Implement ambient intelligence that continuously monitors and updates briefings silently. Move from point-in-time 'Daily Briefing at 8am' to a Living Briefing that shows 'Last significant update: 14 minutes ago'. Implement confidence decay where older info fades visually while new info pulses. Auto-archive stale news, surface fresh signals automatically.
acceptance_criteria:
- Briefing updates continuously in background
- Shows time since last significant update
- Visual confidence decay for aging information
- Pulse animation on new high-signal content
- Auto-archive items older than 24h
- Badge: '3 new developments since you last checked'

title: Explainable AI Transparency Layer
description: Every AI summary must show: (1) Number of sources with inline snippet previews, (2) Confidence score percentage, (3) 'Why this matters to you' personalized context based on user's watchlist and portfolio, (4) 'Show me the contrary view' button that surfaces opposing analysis, (5) Source diversity indicator (analysts vs community vs news vs social). Users want to understand AI reasoning, not just summaries.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every digest card shows: sources count, confidence %, personalized relevance
- Expandable inline source snippets without leaving app
- Contrary view button finds bearish takes on bullish news
- Source type badges: Analyst, Community, News, Social
- 'Why this matters' contextual explanation per user

title: Predictive Action Suggestions
description: AI suggests proactive actions based on content: (1) Add related tickers to watchlist, (2) Set price alerts when sentiment shifts detected, (3) Read opposing viewpoints from analysts with correct prediction history, (4) Save to 'Investment Thesis' document, (5) Schedule follow-up reminder. Move from passive reading to active intelligence. Users want better decisions, not more information.
acceptance_criteria:
- Contextual action chips on each briefing card
- Smart watchlist suggestions based on mentioned tickers
- Alert suggestions when sentiment crosses thresholds
- 'Follow this analyst' when they have good track record
- 'Add to thesis' saves to personal research doc
- One-tap action execution

title: Highlight + Ask Interaction Model
description: Enable users to select any text in an AI summary and ask contextual questions: define terms, clarify statements, request elaboration, or challenge assertions. Multi-turn conversation remembers context. Contextual questioning makes AI collaborative, not replacement. Reference previous questions: 'Tell me more about the CEO change' → 'What's the market reaction?' → 'Should I be worried?'
acceptance_criteria:
- Text selection triggers 'Ask about this' context menu
- Define, elaborate, challenge, compare action types
- Inline AI response without leaving briefing view
- Conversation thread per briefing item
- Cross-reference with previous user questions
- Natural follow-up: 'What about X?' maintains context

title: Bookmarks & Organization System
description: Address Syft user feedback: 'Desperately needs bookmarks'. Users want to save important AI summaries and organize them into folders/collections. Implement smart collections that auto-organize by ticker, topic, or date. Enable sharing saved insights with commentary to investment groups. Export collections as PDF research reports.
acceptance_criteria:
- Save any briefing card to bookmarks
- Create custom folders/collections
- Smart auto-collections: by ticker, topic, sentiment
- Share bookmarks with personal commentary
- Export collection as PDF research report
- Full-text search across saved items

title: Voice-First Interface & Audio Briefings
description: Implement voice announcements and audio briefings. Users can listen to morning briefing while commuting. Natural language queries: 'What did I read about NVDA yesterday?' AI recalls and summarizes verbally. Voice-first for hands-free usage. Multi-modal memory with voice notes.
acceptance_criteria:
- Audio briefing with natural TTS
- Voice query interface
- Recall previous briefings by voice
- Hands-free navigation
- CarPlay/background audio support
- Voice note annotations on bookmarks

title: Portfolio Correlation Engine
description: Cross-reference news with user's actual portfolio positions. When AAPL drops 5%, AI shows 'This impacts your 2nd largest holding (12% of portfolio)'. Calculate correlation risk. Alert when multiple holdings face same sector headwinds. Personalized impact analysis: dollar value impact, risk exposure warnings.
acceptance_criteria:
- Import portfolio positions (manual or broker API)
- Real-time correlation analysis
- Impact quantification: dollar value, % of portfolio
- Sector concentration alerts
- Risk heatmap visualization
- 'What if' scenario modeling

title: Refactor BriefingView to LivingBriefingView with continuous update paradigm
description: Replace refresh button with continuous background updates. Implement visual indicator showing 'Last significant update: X minutes ago'. Auto-refresh when app returns from background. Remove manual refresh paradigm.

title: Implement Confidence Decay visual system with aging indicators
description: Older information fades visually (opacity reduction). New high-signal content pulses with animation. Implement age-based color coding: fresh (green), aging (yellow), stale (gray). Auto-archive items after 24 hours.

title: Create background fetch service for ambient intelligence
description: Implement silent background updates when app is in foreground. Update BriefingViewModel to poll for new developments every 5 minutes. Badge system: '3 new developments since you last checked'.

title: Build expandable transparency card with sources inline
description: Every digest card has expandable 'Sources & Confidence' section. Show source favicons and brief snippet. Display confidence percentage. Implement smooth expand/collapse animation.

title: Implement 'Why This Matters' personalized context engine
description: Generate personalized relevance explanation based on user's watchlist and reading history. Example: 'You follow NVDA and have 3 bullish positions. This news may impact your portfolio by ~2%.'

title: Create 'Show Contrary View' button with opposing analysis
description: Button surfaces bearish takes on bullish news and vice versa. AI finds analysts with opposing viewpoints. Display contrary view inline or in modal.

title: Apple Intelligence On-Device Summarization
description: Implement on-device AI summarization using iOS 26+ Apple Intelligence. Zero API costs, complete privacy. Uses UIWritingToolsCoordinator for Apple Intelligence (iPhone 15 Pro+), falls back to Core ML models for older devices. Integrate Natural Language framework for key point extraction and sentiment analysis.
acceptance_criteria:
- Apple Intelligence Writing Tools integration for iOS 26+ devices
- Core ML fallback for universal device support
- Natural Language framework for sentiment analysis
- Zero API costs - all processing on-device
- Neural Engine utilization for speed
- Complete privacy - no data leaves device

title: Create OnDeviceSummarizationService with Writing Tools integration
description: Build service that uses UIWritingToolsCoordinator for AI summarization on iPhone 15 Pro+ devices. Handle availability checks, configuration (tone/length), and error handling.

title: Implement Core ML fallback for older devices
description: Create Core ML model integration for devices without Apple Intelligence. Use lightweight summarization model (~200MB) downloaded on first use.

title: Natural Language key point extraction
description: Use NLEmbedding and NLTagger for semantic key point extraction. Score sentences by centrality and return top N most important points.
